pr.title: mn: match mnitemsw, mnstagesw, and mnvibration functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Draft/WIP PR adding menu settings decomp work for mnitemsw, mnstagesw, and mnvibration. The diff adds implementations and headers for item/stage switch menu functions, expands mnvibration prototypes/types, and changes some raw GObj flag access to HSD_GObjProc flags_3. Bot output reported 7 new 100% matches and 3 partial improvements, but reviewer comments challenged a GObj wrapper type and an apparent function deletion; the author acknowledged the mnvibration area was still WIP.

key_files.role: Added implementations for mnItemSw_80233A98, mnItemSw_8023405C, and mnItemSw_802358C0 using ItemSwTable, linked-node traversal, menu flow updates, and GObjProc creation.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists mnItemSw_80233A98 and mnItemSw_8023405C as 100% matches; mnItemSw_802358C0 as 92.10%

key_files.role: Added header guard, ItemSwTable typedef, prototypes, and externs for mnitemsw symbols.
Added implementations for mnStageSw_8023593C, mnStageSw_802364A0, and mnStageSw_80237410, including temporary struct views, node traversal, stage table iteration, and menu flow/GObjProc setup.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists mnStageSw_8023593C, mnStageSw_802364A0, and mnStageSw_80237410 as 100% matches

key_files.role: Added header guard, prototypes, and externs for mnstagesw symbols.
Modified mnvibration implementation, added mnVibration_802474C4, fn_80248084, fn_80248748, revised mnVibration_80249174, and reorganized includes/types; this file drew all review feedback.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists mnVibration_802474C4 and fn_80248084 as 100% matches; fn_80248748 and mnVibration_80249174 as partial improvements

key_files.role: Expanded header with VibNode, VibNodeContainer, VibData, VibJObjData, concrete prototypes for several functions, and externs for mnVibration_803EECEC and mnVibration_804D6C28.

what_changed: Implemented mnItemSw_80233A98 with a switch on item index and language-dependent JP/non-JP return paths for cases 0x11 and 0x13.
Implemented linked-list style node lookup helpers mnItemSw_8023405C, mnStageSw_802364A0, and mnVibration_802474C4 using offset-described temporary node/data structs.
Implemented mnStageSw_8023593C as a do-while loop over mnStageSw_803ED4C4 with calls to gm_801641CC, gm_80164430, and gm_801641E4.

Implemented menu transition entrypoints mnItemSw_802358C0, mnStageSw_80237410, and revised mnVibration_80249174 to update mn_804A04F0 menu flow state, create a GObj, attach a proc, and set proc->flags_3.
Added or refined headers for mnitemsw, mnstagesw, and mnvibration with prototypes, externs, and temporary struct definitions.
Bot report claimed 7 new 100% function matches and 3 improvements, but the PR body and author review comment both identify the PR as WIP.
smart_moves: The diff replaces raw byte manipulation of a GObj flags offset in mnVibration_80249174 with HSD_GObjProc* proc and proc->flags_3 assignment, which aligns with typed-field cleanup when the proc type is available.
Several empty or placeholder menu source/header files were given narrow implementations plus matching headers, making the changed symbols searchable by their real menu TU paths.
The added item/stage/vibration node traversal helpers use small temporary structs to express repeated x8/x10 linked-node access rather than leaving all access as raw pointer arithmetic.
The diff keeps ordinary asset-name strings such as MnVibTitle_Top_joint and MnVibPanel_Top_shapeanim_joint inline in the lbArchive_LoadSections call instead of converting them to named data symbols.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matching attempt exposes a known HSD type or proc field, prefer the typed access over raw offset byte manipulation; this PR's mnVibration_80249174 changed a raw GObj flags byte update into proc->flags_3 after HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54.
For repeated menu node traversal, temporary local node/data structs can make x8 next-pointer and x10 child/head-pointer patterns reviewable while names remain offset-based until semantics are proven.

Do not treat draft matching code as review-ready when it deletes or restructures existing code without clear explanation; the reviewer explicitly questioned an apparent whole-function deletion in mnvibration.c and the author acknowledged WIP cleanup was pending.

naming_conventions.pattern: Menu-setting functions use subsystem prefixes matching the file: mnItemSw_*, mnStageSw_*, and mnVibration_*.
Unknown callback-style functions remain fn_<address> where semantics are not recovered.
Temporary recovered structs in this PR use subsystem/type names but retain offset field names such as x8, x10, x2C, x34, and x50 when field semantics are not established.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural loops for table and node traversal
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: mnStageSw_8023593C uses a do-while loop over 0x1D entries; mnItemSw_8023405C, mnStageSw_802364A0, and mnVibration_802474C4 use for loops to advance linked nodes by index.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Temporary padded structs to express known offsets
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: ItemSwData, StageSw_NodeData, VibData, VibNodeContainer, and VibJObjData encode offsets needed by the matched functions. Reviewer concern on a GObj wrapper shows these wrappers should be justified and removed when a canonical type exists.
mnStageSw_8023593C declares s32 dummy[3]; (void) dummy;. This is evidence of a matching tactic, not an endorsed style, and would normally need narrow verifier justification.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Function pointer cast around incomplete prototype
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: mnVibration_80249174 calls ((void (*)(int)) mnVibration_80248ED4)(arg0) while mnVibration_80248ED4 remains UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in the header, indicating a local cast used around an unresolved signature.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Why are we making a wrapper type for a GObj instead of using it directly?
review_feedback.interpretation: Reviewer questioned a temporary GObj wrapper type and implied direct use of HSD_GObj may be preferable when the real type is known.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Very confused why an entire function was deleted?
review_feedback.interpretation: Reviewer flagged an unexplained deletion/replacement in mnvibration.c during a WIP rewrite.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.comment: I shoulda called out that this one is still a WIP - will clean this stuff up
review_feedback.interpretation: Author acknowledged the mnvibration changes were not final and needed cleanup.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for mnvibration.c cleanup after PR2077, especially VibGObj/GObj wrapper removal or direct HSD_GObj use.
Search for mnItemSw_802358C0 and fn_80248748 in later PRs to see whether the partial matches from this bot report were completed.
Search for mnVibration_80248ED4 signature recovery to remove the function pointer cast in mnVibration_80249174.
Search for StageSw_GObj, ItemSwData, StageSw_NodeData, VibData, and VibJObjData to see whether temporary offset structs were promoted, renamed, or replaced by canonical types.
Search review history for dummy[3] or stack dummy locals in mnStageSw_8023593C to determine whether the stack-shape tactic survived review.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata and changed files
notes: The slice includes full diff and review comments, plus a bot match report naming exact matched and partially improved functions.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so this intake does not treat the changes as merged or accepted.
The PR was draft/WIP according to raw_pr_json and the PR body; author explicitly said cleanup was pending.
Review feedback exists only on mnvibration.c; no reviewer comments are loaded for mnitemsw.c, mnitemsw.h, mnstagesw.c, or mnstagesw.h.
No local build logs, objdiff artifacts beyond the bot summary, or final resolution comments are included in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use real GObj/HSD types instead of wrapper structs when available
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2077 review questioned creating a wrapper type for a GObj in mnvibration.c: 'Why are we making a wrapper type for a GObj instead of using it directly?' Candidate lesson: temporary offset wrappers are acceptable only while recovering types; prefer the real HSD_GObj or related typed field when known.

accepted_candidate_records.title: WIP matching PRs should explain broad deletions or rewrites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2077's mnvibration rewrite drew review confusion over an apparent entire-function deletion, and the author responded that the work was still WIP and would be cleaned up. Candidate lesson: when replacing/deleting existing decomp code during a match attempt, state the rationale and keep reviewable cleanup boundaries clear.

source_update_candidates.title: Discourage wrapper structs over known GObj types
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note under typed-fields-over-pointer-math or matching-tactics-need-evidence: if the object is known to be HSD_GObj or another canonical baselib type, do not introduce a padded wrapper solely to reach a field offset; use the canonical type/field or document why the wrapper is temporary and necessary.
source_update_candidates.reason: Reviewer explicitly challenged a GObj wrapper type; this aligns with existing typed-field guidance but may be worth a more specific note.

source_update_candidates.title: Function-pointer casts around UNK prototypes should be treated as temporary
source_update_candidates.text: Consider clarifying that casts such as ((void (*)(int)) symbol)(arg) around UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS are matching bridge code and should trigger follow-up signature recovery in the owning header when the body or callsite proves the signature.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnvibration.h keeps mnVibration_80248ED4 as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR shows a concrete instance of a cast used to bypass an unresolved header signature; no review comment addresses it, so route as candidate only.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the bot-reported matches as accepted project state because the PR is closed unmerged and marked WIP.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the loaded comments; only the GObj wrapper and apparent function deletion were explicitly questioned.
Do not add path facts claiming these menu structs are canonical; the PR uses offset-based temporary names and lacks final cleanup evidence.
Do not treat dummy[3] or function-pointer casts as endorsed tactics; the loaded standards require verifier evidence and cleanup rationale, which are not present beyond the bot summary.
